tests_run: 88 passed (78 pre-existing + 10 new A4-R1 prompt-contract tests)
manual_local_model_smoke: Not performed in this automated run. Required after commit: (1) gemma4:12b-it-qat: 'che cos'e una pompa?' -> should use general knowledge, not refuse for lack of context. If still refuses, flag as adapter/template/QAT issue, not model quality. (2) qwen3:14b: BlueRev/R203/P101 question -> must not invent project memory; should state assumptions. (3) mistral-small3.2:24b: persona request then technical question -> must not claim persistence ('da ora in poi').
known_residual_risks: A4-R1 is a behavioral instruction, not a deterministic enforcement barrier. A sufficiently disobedient or misaligned model may still fabricate project facts or false persistence claims despite the prompt.
The public/general vs project-specific boundary is model-side judgment: models may err in both directions (too restrictive or too inventive).

Preference/persona requests ('chiamami Signore') remain allowed as session-only; the deterministic gate does not intercept them. False persistence risk mitigated by the no-persistence clause, but not eliminated.
Italian memory-write and document/IP-write intents not yet detected by the operational detector. Deferred to A5-R2.
Model benchmark comparisons remain invalid until manual smoke with fixed prompt is completed for all candidate models.
Gemma refusal behavior may be adapter/template/QAT issue rather than model quality; A4-R1 is necessary but may not be sufficient to enable Gemma correctly.
